circular.title: Master Circular - Exposure Norms and Statutory / Other Restrictions - UCBs
circular.summary: This Master Circular consolidates prudential instructions for Urban Co-operative Banks to manage credit risk and avoid concentration. It mandates exposure ceilings for individual and group borrowers linked to Tier-I capital, sets specific targets for Small Value Loans to promote financial inclusion, and establishes limits on real estate and unsecured advances. Furthermore, it details statutory and regulatory prohibitions, notably the ban on lending to directors, their relatives, or against the bank's own shares, ensuring robust governance and financial stability within the co-operative banking sector.

clauses.text: As a prudential measure aimed at better risk management and avoidance of concentration of credit risk, UCBs have been advised to fix limits on their exposure to borrowers and specific sectors.
clauses.summary: UCBs must fix exposure limits to manage credit risk

clauses.text: Banks must observe statutory and regulatory restrictions regarding advances against and investments in shares, debentures, and bonds.
clauses.summary: UCBs must observe statutory and regulatory restrictions

clauses.text: Tier-I capital as on March 31 of the preceding financial year shall be reckoned for fixing exposure limits.
clauses.summary: Tier-I capital definition for exposure limits

clauses.text: Includes funded/non-funded limits, equipment leasing, hire purchase financing, and ad hoc limits.
The decision regarding the definition of a group is left to the UCB's perception based on commonality of management and effective control.

Includes clean overdrafts and loans against personal security but excludes advances backed by government guarantees, supply bills drawn on government, and the secured portion of partly secured advances.

With Board approval, fix ceilings so that: (i) Exposure to an individual borrower does not exceed 15% of Tier-I capital. (ii) Exposure to a group of connected borrowers does not exceed 25% of Tier-I capital.
clauses.summary: Exposure ceilings for individual and group borrowers

clauses.text: UCBs shall have at least 50% of aggregate loans comprising Small Value Loans (value ≤ ₹25 lakh or 0.4% of Tier-I capital, whichever is higher), subject to a ceiling of ₹3 crore per borrower.
Aggregate exposure to residential mortgages (excluding priority sector) shall not exceed 25% of total loans and advances.
Aggregate exposure to the real estate sector (excluding housing loans to individuals) shall not exceed 5% of total loans and advances.
Individual/Group limits depend on CRAR and DTL: * CRAR ≥ 9%: Limits range from ₹1 lakh to ₹5 lakh depending on DTL. * CRAR < 9%: Limits range from ₹0.25 lakh to ₹2 lakh depending on DTL.
Total unsecured advances should not exceed 10% of total assets as per the audited balance sheet of the preceding March 31.
In terms of Section 20(1)(a) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, UCBs cannot grant loans and advances on the security of their own shares.
clauses.summary: Prohibition on lending against own shares

clauses.text: UCBs shall not, except with prior RBI approval, remit any debt due to it by past/present directors or firms in which they are interested.
clauses.summary: Prohibition on remitting debt to directors

clauses.text: UCBs shall not make, provide, or renew any loans/advances to directors or their relatives, or to firms/companies in which they are interested.
clauses.summary: Prohibition on lending to directors and relatives

clauses.text: Prohibited for stock brokers and commodity brokers.
clauses.summary: Prohibition on bank finance against shares, debentures, and bonds for stock brokers and commodity brokers

clauses.text: Individual loans limited to ₹5 lakh (physical) and ₹10 lakh (demat).
clauses.summary: Limit on individual loans against shares, debentures, and bonds
Margin requirement for loans against shares, debentures, and bonds

clauses.text: Total aggregate of such loans shall not exceed 20% of Tier-I capital.
clauses.summary: Aggregate limit for loans against shares, debentures, and bonds

obligations.text: Reckon Tier-I capital as on March 31 of the preceding financial year for fixing exposure limits.
obligations.plain_english: Calculate Tier-I capital for exposure limits

obligations.text: Limit individual borrower exposure to 15% of Tier-I capital.
Limit group borrower exposure to 25% of Tier-I capital.
Ensure at least 50% of aggregate loans comprise Small Value Loans (≤ ₹25 lakh or 0.4% of Tier-I capital).
Restrict aggregate residential mortgage exposure (non-priority sector) to 25% of total assets.
Restrict aggregate real estate sector exposure (excluding housing loans to individuals) to 5% of total loans and advances.
Limit total unsecured loans and advances to 10% of total assets as per the preceding year's audited balance sheet.
Maintain a 50% margin on all advances against the security of shares and debentures.
obligations.plain_english: Maintain margin on loans against shares and debentures

obligations.text: Restrict loans against shares/debentures to ₹5 lakh (physical form) and ₹10 lakh (demat form).
obligations.plain_english: Limit loans against shares and debentures

prohibitions.text: Granting of any loans or advances against the security of the bank's own shares.
Loans, advances, or financial accommodation to directors, their relatives, or entities where they are interested.
prohibitions.plain_english: No lending to directors or relatives

prohibitions.text: Bank finance against shares, debentures, and bonds for stock brokers and commodity brokers.
prohibitions.plain_english: No bank finance for stock brokers and commodity brokers

penalties.condition: Granting of loans or advances against the security of the bank's own shares
Loans, advances, or financial accommodation to directors, their relatives, or entities where they are interested

definitions.definition: Capital reckoned as of March 31 of the preceding financial year for exposure limit purposes
Sum of funded and non-funded credit limits, underwriting, and leasing/hire-purchase facilities
Borrowing units linked by commonality of management and effective control
Loans without tangible security, excluding specific government-backed or high-liquidity instrument-backed advances

references.circular_number: Master Circular dated April 1, 2025 on Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy
Master Direction on Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio of UCBs dated April 1, 2023
Master Direction – Credit Card and Debit Card – Issuance and Conduct Directions, 2022
Master Direction – Reserve Bank of India (Filing of Supervisory Returns) Directions – 2024
